mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a labor market tightening that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.4 million signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a monetary-supply driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in monetary-supply driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 20 billion signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% and a 2-month increase in initial claims above 250 thousand signals a labor market tightening that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 250 thousand for 2 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 100 and a 2-month increase in housing starts above 1.4 million signals a supply-demand driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 100 and HOUST > 1.4 million for 2 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-demand driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
